  "findings": [
    "Click-type torque wrench used in reverse/upside down direction without verification of bi-directional calibration",
    "Use of uncalibrated torque wrench violating OEM and engineering specifications"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt the fastening process immediately",
    "Acquire a calibrated torque wrench certified for the required direction of operation",
    "Loosen and re-torque all fasteners tightened during this session to the specified OEM torque values"
  ],
  "rationale": "Using a click-type torque wrench upside down or in reverse often bypasses the internal mechanism's calibrated release, and using an uncalibrated tool guarantees inaccurate torque application. This introduces a high risk of joint failure, over-tensioning, or under-tensioning on an operating asset.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Torque Specifications",
    "ASME B107.300 (Torque Instruments)",
    "ISO 6789 (Assembly tools for screws and nuts - Hand torque tools)"
  ]
}
